name: react-byok
description: Run a no-tool-calling LLM as a ReAct agent via VS Code agent hooks.

commands.title: ReAct BYOK: Set up in this workspace
ReAct BYOK: Convene council of experts

walkthroughs.title: Get started with ReAct BYOK
walkthroughs.description: Run a no-tool-calling model as a ReAct agent. Set it up, point it at your model, and pick the path that works on your machine (hooks, or hook-free for locked-down/OneDrive Windows).

steps.title: Set up this workspace
steps.description:
Stage the runtime and write the config files.
[Run setup](command:react-byok.setup)

steps.title: Point it at your model
steps.description:
Set your OpenAI-compatible endpoint, key, and model in council.json.
[Open model config](command:react-byok.openConfig)

steps.title: Check your environment
steps.description:
See whether the in-chat hooks will work here, or whether to use the hook-free runner.
[Diagnose setup](command:react-byok.doctor)

steps.title: Path A — in-chat agent (hooks)
steps.description:
Enable the hooks preview, then pick the ReAct BYOK agent in chat.
[Enable hooks setting](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%22chat.useCustomAgentHooks%22)

steps.title: Path B — hook-free runner (no cmd.exe / OneDrive)
steps.description:
Run the agent without hooks — it spawns directly and writes ANSWER.md.
[Run agent (hook-free)](command:react-byok.run)

scripts.build: npm run typecheck && npm run bundle
scripts.package: npm run build && vsce package --out react-byok.vsix
